questions.prompt_text: An AI agent operating in a Retail Inventory Management system processes 50 tasks per minute. How many tasks will it complete in 3 hours, assuming zero downtime?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Convert hours to minutes: 3 hours × 60 minutes/hour = 180 minutes. Step 2: Multiply tasks per minute by total minutes: 50 tasks/minute × 180 minutes = 9,000 tasks.

questions.prompt_text: In a Financial Fraud Detection agentic pipeline, Tool A costs $0.48 per call and achieves 65% accuracy. Tool B costs $0.38 per call and achieves 86% accuracy. If the pipeline makes 4464 calls, what is the cost-per-accurate-result for each tool, and which tool is more cost-efficient?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Tool A: accurate_results_A = 4464 × (65 / 100) = 4464 × 0.65 = 2,901.6 accurate results. total_cost_A = 4464 × $0.48 = $2,142.72. cost_per_accurate_A = $2,142.72 / 2,901.6 = ~$0.7385 per accurate result. Tool B: accurate_results_B = 4464 × (86 / 100) = 4464 × 0.86 = 3,839.04 accurate results. total_cost_B = 4464 × $0.38 = $1,696.32. cost_per_accurate_B = $1,696.32 / 3,839.04 = ~$0.4419 per accurate result. Comparison: $0.4419 < $0.7385, so Tool B is more cost-efficient.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Tool A: ~$0.74 per accurate result; Tool B: ~$0.44 per accurate result. Tool B is more cost-efficient.

questions.prompt_text: A Space Exploration Data Processing workflow requires 8 agents to each complete a sub-task. Each agent takes 11.7 seconds. If run sequentially, what is the total latency? If run in parallel (ignoring overhead), what is the total latency? What is the time saved by parallelization?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Sequential latency = NUM_AGENTS × AGENT_LATENCY_SECONDS = 8 × 11.7 = 93.6 seconds. Step 2: Parallel latency = AGENT_LATENCY_SECONDS = 11.7 seconds (all agents run simultaneously). Step 3: Time saved = sequential_latency − parallel_latency = 93.6 − 11.7 = 81.9 seconds. Step 4: Speedup factor = 93.6 / 11.7 = 8×.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Sequential latency: 93.6 seconds; Parallel latency: 11.7 seconds; Time saved: 81.9 seconds; Speedup factor: 8×

questions.prompt_text: A Climate Data Analysis RAG agent has a knowledge base of 3267 documents. A similarity threshold of 0.69 (on a 0–1 scale) filters retrieved results. In a test run, 1025 documents exceeded the threshold. What percentage of the knowledge base was retrieved? If each retrieved document costs $0.044 to process, what is the total retrieval cost?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Retrieval percentage = (RETRIEVED_DOCS / TOTAL_DOCUMENTS) × 100 = (1025 / 3267) × 100 = 0.31376 × 100 = ~31.38%. Step 2: Total retrieval cost = RETRIEVED_DOCS × COST_PER_DOC = 1025 × $0.044 = $45.10.

questions.prompt_text: An Autonomous Vehicle Navigation agentic pipeline has 5 sequential steps. Each step has an independent success rate of 96%. What is the probability (as a percentage) that the entire pipeline completes without error? If 514 independent runs are executed, how many successful end-to-end completions are expected?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Convert success rate to decimal: 96 / 100 = 0.96. Step 2: Pipeline success probability = 0.96^5. 0.96^2 = 0.9216; 0.96^3 = 0.884736; 0.96^4 = 0.84934656; 0.96^5 = 0.8153726976. Step 3: Pipeline success percentage = 0.8153726976 × 100 = ~81.54%. Step 4: Expected successful runs = NUM_RUNS × pipeline_success_probability = 514 × 0.8153726976 = ~419.10 runs.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Pipeline success probability: ~81.54%; Expected successful completions: ~419.10 runs (approximately 419 out of 514)
